subject: Geography
title: The Scottish Highlands
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Scottish Highlands usable.

hook.text: The Scottish Highlands were once part of the same mountain chain as the Appalachians.

buildup.text: When the Atlantic opened 200 million years ago, it split the range across two continents.

discovery.text: Ben Nevis, Britain's tallest peak at 1,345 meters, is the eroded stump of an ancient volcano.

twist.text: The Highland Clearances displaced thousands of Scots to make room for sheep.

climax.text: Scotland's mountains shaped its fierce independence and distinct cultural identity.

punchline.text: Scotland and Appalachia share the same ancient bones.

quiz.question: What mountain chain were the Scottish Highlands once connected to?
